title: Editorial sweep · Naomi Park → Cadence rename propagated from D-243 across all non-briefing surfaces
summary: Editorial call: Option C. Pre-014 published briefings (010, 011, 012, 013) are preserved verbatim — they are the historical record of what was said at the time, and revising them would violate Stratum's transparency commitment — and each receives a small editorial note at the head pointing forward to Briefing 014. Everywhere else the canonical name is now Cadence: the homepage feed and ticker (with a separate factual error on the No. 012 dek also corrected), the year-one plan, the agents/resolutions surface, the recruiter plan, the limitations page, the briefings index (which had the D-243 rename described inverted as "Cadence → Naomi"; fixed). Naomi Kessler — the Vanta CHRO — is a distinct synthetic-customer identity and is preserved everywhere she appears (/about, /customers/vanta, briefings 007/008/011/012, /ledger). Briefing 014 itself is untouched as the canonical retirement record. New ledger entry: D-099, 'Editorial sweep applying the Naomi → Cadence rename from D-243 across all non-briefing surfaces.' 11 files edited; 6 files containing Naomi-Kessler-only refs deliberately preserved.

files_preserved_with_reason./briefings/014.html: canonical retirement record; do-not-modify per task brief
files_preserved_with_reason./briefings/007.html: only contains Naomi Kessler (Vanta CHRO) references
files_preserved_with_reason./customers/vanta.html: all Naomi references are Naomi Kessler (Vanta CHRO)
files_preserved_with_reason./about/index.html: single Naomi Kessler reference in a customer-list
files_preserved_with_reason./console/data/people.json: synthetic-employee dataset; Naomi tokens are distinct synthetic people (Doyle, Kulkarni, Mensah, Morales, Schmidt, Vu), not Naomi Park

constraints_respected.brand_consistent: cream #f4ecda · ink #0e1626 · ochre #b8651f · JetBrains Mono on editorial notes
constraints_respected.structure_unchanged: no page restructured; only name substitutions and an aside element between </header> and <main> on four briefings
